text.title: PM Experimentation Practices
text.summary: Behavioral hypothesis framing, pre-registration requirements, decision rules, and the failure modes that prevent experiments from producing genuine organizational learning.

rule.statement: Every experiment hypothesis must follow the if-then-because structure: "If [behavior change], then [metric M] will [move direction] by [delta], because [mechanism]." Reject solution-shaped hypotheses that name a feature instead of a behavior change.
rule.rationale: Solution-shaped hypotheses ("add a banner") carry the answer in the question, preventing genuine learning. Behavioral if-then-because forces articulation of the causal mechanism, which can be falsified.

example.note: Hypothesis: "We should add a progress bar to onboarding."
Failure: names a feature, not a behavior. If the experiment fails, you learn nothing about the underlying problem.

Hypothesis: "If we show users a 3-step progress indicator during onboarding, then Day-7 activation will increase by >= 4% (relative), because users who see how close they are to completion are less likely to abandon mid-flow."
Primary metric: d7_activation_rate. MDE: 4% relative. Decision rule: SHIP if lift >= 4% with no guardrail breach; ITERATE if 0-4% lift; KILL if <= 0% or guardrail breach.

rule.statement: Pre-register the decision rule before any traffic is exposed. The rule must name the primary metric, the threshold, guardrail metrics, and what to do if guardrails breach. A decision rule that allows "we'll see" is not a rule.
rule.rationale: Without a pre-committed decision rule, the ship/kill call becomes negotiable post-readout; HiPPO override rates are near 100% when no rule exists.

rule.statement: Pre-register segment cuts — maximum 5 — before launch. Post-hoc segment analysis is exploratory only and labelled as such; it never drives a ship decision.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc segment cuts are p-hacking by another name. Pre-registration is the only defense against finding a "winning" segment after 47 cuts.

antipattern.description: HiPPO override: a clean readout is available but leadership ships the loser because the experiment contradicts a prior conviction.
antipattern.reason: Without a written decision rule co-signed by the exec sponsor before launch, "the data wins" is aspirational, not operational.

antipattern.description: Local maxima: 18 months of CTA color tests yield 6% cumulative lift and zero strategic learning.
antipattern.reason: Without an opportunity-tree-rooted experiment slate, experimentation drifts to micro-optimizations that do not compound into competitive advantage.

antipattern.description: Reading "p=0.08, directionally positive" as a ship signal.
antipattern.reason: Inconclusive results (power < 0.7 or p > alpha) must produce "extend or kill," never "ship directionally." The pre-registered decision rule handles this automatically when applied verbatim.

antipattern.description: Treating null results as failures and suppressing them from the learning repository.
antipattern.reason: A pattern of nulls in a region of the opportunity tree means rethink the opportunity. Suppressed nulls cause the same failed experiment to be re-run by the next PM cycle.

text.reference: Full hypothesis intake schema with if-then-because, primary metric, guardrails, decision rule, and stop conditions.
30-line Python: scores idea testability and routes to tier (A/B, prototype, qual, irreversible-strategic).
